blockingIssues.description: Line 107: ExtractionConfig::default() sets store_path to '.skills' but AGENTS.md specifies user-extensible skills should be in '.swell/skills/'. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 20).
Line 139: TrajectoryAnalyzer has #[allow(dead_code)] attribute and its store field is never used for database lookups. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 20).
Line 955: Confidence calculation uses sum of all pattern frequencies (frequency_map.values().sum()) as denominator instead of per-pattern frequency. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 20).

Line 1145: Convention parsing uses wrong string slice index [3..] instead of [4..]. For input '- **name**: description', position 0='-', 1=' ', 2='*', 3='*', 4='n'. Name starts at index 4. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 20).
Line 1170: get_anti_patterns_for_error is an unimplemented stub returning empty vec with comment 'For MVP, return empty'. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 20).
blockingIssues.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/pattern_learning.rs:1170-1172: Ok(Vec::new()) with 'For MVP, return empty' comment

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: CRITICAL: 5 blocking issues have persisted for 20 rounds without resolution across 2 features (memory-skill-extraction, memory-pattern-learning). These are well-identified, localized bugs with exact line numbers and clear fix instructions. Validators pass but correctness bugs remain. The orchestrator created no fix feature - the suggested fix-memory-blocking-issues was never implemented. HIGH-PRIORITY: Assign a dedicated worker to fix-memory-blocking-issues with explicit requirement to fix each listed issue at the exact lines specified. Do not allow further scrutiny re-runs until the fix feature is completed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 5 issues marked 'STILL NOT FIXED since round 1' with file:line evidence. No code changes made to address these issues across 20 rounds. Workers correctly implement features but introduce logic bugs that tests don't catch.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Blocking issues are pure code correctness bugs that do not cause test failures. Consider adding specific validation checks or stricter code review requirements for features that have known blocking issues. Current scrutiny loop cycles without resolution because workers follow the same Rust skill procedure and produce the same buggy code.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature builds and tests pass but correctness bugs persist (wrong slice index, unused field, wrong denominator). The 42 swell-memory tests pass despite these bugs.
